name: github mcp tool-selection matrix
description: Compare stock and tuned tool descriptions for common GitHub MCP Server repository, issue, pull request, and Actions workflows.

instruction_variants.instructions: Choose the single GitHub MCP tool that should be called next. Use write tools only when the user explicitly asks to create, update, comment, or open something. Prefer exact-id read tools when an issue, pull request, workflow run, or job id is provided. Prefer search tools when the user does not know the exact id or path.
instruction_variants.name: github_mcp_host_rules

ref.description: Accepts optional git refs such as refs/tags/{tag}, refs/heads/{branch} or refs/pull/{pr_number}/head

sha.description: Accepts optional commit SHA. If specified, it will be used instead of ref

tools.name: get_file_contents
tools.purpose: Get the contents of a file or directory from a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query (GitHub code search REST). Implicit AND between terms; supports OR, NOT, and quoted phrases. Qualifiers include repo:owner/repo, org:, user:, language:, path:, filename:, extension:, in:file, in:path, size:, is:archived, is:fork.

tools.name: search_code
tools.purpose: Fast and precise code search across ALL GitHub repositories using GitHub's native search engine. Best for finding exact symbols, functions, classes, or specific code patterns.

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues
tools.purpose: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

method.description: The read operation to perform on a single issue: get, get_comments, get_sub_issues, get_parent, get_labels

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read
tools.purpose: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

tools.name: create_issue
tools.purpose: Create a new issue in a GitHub repository with a title and optional body.

tools.name: add_issue_comment
tools.purpose: Add a comment to a specific issue in a GitHub repository. Use this tool to add comments to pull requests as well (in this case pass pull request number as issue_number), but only if user is not asking specifically to add review comments.

head.description: Filter by head user/org and branch

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sort.description: Sort by created, updated, popularity, or long-running

tools.name: list_pull_requests
tools.purpose: List pull requests in a GitHub repository. If the user specifies an author, then DO NOT use this tool and use the search_pull_requests tool instead.

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only pull requests for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub pull request search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only pull requests for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_pull_requests
tools.purpose: Search for pull requests in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:pr

after.description: Cursor for pagination, used only by get_review_comments

method.description: Action to retrieve PR data: get, get_diff, get_status, get_files, get_commits, get_review_comments, get_reviews, get_comments, get_check_runs

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: pull_request_read
tools.purpose: Get information on a specific pull request in GitHub repository.

reviewers.description: GitHub usernames or ORG/team-slug team reviewers

show_ui.description: Whether to render the MCP App form instead of executing immediately

tools.name: create_pull_request
tools.purpose: Create a new pull request in a GitHub repository.

method.description: Method: get_workflow, get_workflow_run, get_workflow_job, download_workflow_run_artifact, get_workflow_run_usage, get_workflow_run_logs_url

resource_id.description: Unique identifier of the workflow, workflow run, job, or artifact resource

tools.name: actions_get
tools.purpose: Get details about specific GitHub Actions resources. Use this tool to get details about individual workflows, workflow runs, jobs, and artifacts by their unique IDs.

failed_only.description: When true, gets logs for all failed jobs in run_id

job_id.description: The unique identifier of the workflow job

return_content.description: Returns actual log content instead of URLs

tail_lines.description: Number of lines to return from the end of the log

tools.name: get_job_logs
tools.purpose: Download logs for a specific workflow job or efficiently get all failed job logs for a workflow run

tools.avoid_when: Avoid when the path is unknown, the user is asking for a symbol/text search, or the task is to discover files across repositories.

path.description: Exact known path to file or directory. Use '/' only for repository root listing.

ref.description: Optional branch, tag, or PR ref when the user asks for a non-default revision.

sha.description: Optional commit SHA; prefer this over ref when the user gives an exact SHA.

tools.name: get_file_contents
tools.purpose: Read one known file or list one known directory in a GitHub repository.
quality_checks: Include owner and repo.
Include path when the user names a path.
Use ref or sha only when the user asks for a specific revision.
tools.use_when: Use when the user already knows the repository and path, such as README.md, package.json, or src/main.go.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid when the user gives an exact path to read or asks about issues, pull requests, Actions, releases, or repository metadata.

query.description: GitHub code search query. Add repo:owner/repo when the repository is known. Use quoted phrases for exact text.

sort.description: Sort field; normally omit unless the user specifies it.

tools.name: search_code
tools.purpose: Find code by symbol, function, class, filename, or text pattern when the exact path is unknown.
quality_checks: Put the symbol or phrase in query.
Scope query with repo:owner/repo when possible.
Do not use this as a file reader.
tools.use_when: Use for questions like where is X implemented, find usages of Y, or search for an exact code pattern.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid when the user gives a specific issue number, asks about pull requests, or asks to create/comment/update an issue.

owner.description: Repository owner when scoping to one repo.

query.description: Issue search query. Include terms, labels, states, or authors. The tool is already scoped to issues.

repo.description: Repository name when scoping to one repo.

tools.name: search_issues
tools.purpose: Search issues by keywords, labels, state, author, or other issue-search qualifiers.
quality_checks: Use issue_read instead when issue_number is known.
Include owner and repo when the repo is known.
tools.use_when: Use when the user wants to find existing issues but does not provide an exact issue number.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid when the task is to search for issues, create an issue, or add a new comment.

method.description: Issue read method: get, get_comments, get_sub_issues, get_parent, get_labels.

tools.name: issue_read
tools.purpose: Read details, comments, labels, parent, or sub-issues for one known issue number.
quality_checks: Set method=get for details.
Set method=get_comments for comments.
Do not use for pull requests.
tools.use_when: Use when owner, repo, and issue number are known and the user asks to inspect issue state or metadata.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid when the user asks to comment on an existing issue, read an issue, or open a pull request.
tools.name: create_issue
tools.purpose: Create a new GitHub issue.
quality_checks: Use only for a new issue.
Include title exactly from the user when supplied.
Include body when supplied.
tools.use_when: Use when the user explicitly asks to file, open, or create an issue.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid when the user asks for review comments on a diff, wants to create a new issue, or only wants to read comments.

issue_number.description: Issue or PR number for a top-level comment.

tools.name: add_issue_comment
tools.purpose: Add a top-level comment to an existing issue or pull request by number.
quality_checks: Use pull request number as issue_number for top-level PR comments.
Use pull_request_read for reading PR comments.
tools.use_when: Use when the user explicitly asks to post or add a top-level comment.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid when the user filters by author, text, label, review state, status text, or any query-like condition; use search_pull_requests then. Avoid when a specific PR number is given.

sort.description: Sort by created, updated, popularity, or long-running.

tools.name: list_pull_requests
tools.purpose: List pull requests in one repository by simple state, base branch, head branch, sort, or pagination filters.
quality_checks: Use search_pull_requests for author or keyword filters.
Use pull_request_read when pullNumber is known.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks to list or browse PRs in a repository without keyword or author search.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for simple state/base/head listing, specific PR numbers, or creating PRs.

owner.description: Repository owner when scoping to one repo.

query.description: Pull request search query. Include author, keywords, state, labels, or review qualifiers. The tool is already scoped to PRs.

repo.description: Repository name when scoping to one repo.

tools.name: search_pull_requests
tools.purpose: Search pull requests by author, keywords, state, label, review, status, or other GitHub PR search qualifiers.
quality_checks: Include owner and repo when known.
Use list_pull_requests for simple state/base/head lists.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks for PRs by author, text, status phrase, review state, or other query-like criteria.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid when the user wants to list/search many PRs, create a PR, or add a top-level PR comment.

method.description: PR read method: get, get_diff, get_status, get_files, get_commits, get_review_comments, get_reviews, get_comments, get_check_runs.

tools.name: pull_request_read
tools.purpose: Read one known pull request's details, diff, files, commits, reviews, comments, status, or check runs.
quality_checks: Use method=get_files for changed files.
Use method=get_diff for patch content.
Use method=get_check_runs for check runs.
Use method=get_review_comments for line review threads.
tools.use_when: Use when owner, repo, and pull request number are known and the user asks to inspect the PR.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid when the user asks to read, list, search, or comment on PRs. Avoid if head/base/title are missing unless using the MCP App form is intended.

show_ui.description: Whether to render the MCP App form before executing.

tools.name: create_pull_request
tools.purpose: Open a new pull request from an existing head branch into a base branch.
quality_checks: Confirm owner, repo, title, head, and base.
Set draft=true when the user asks for a draft.
Use show_ui=true when confirmation or missing fields are needed.
tools.use_when: Use only when the user explicitly asks to create or open a pull request.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid when the user asks for actual log content, failed job log excerpts, or tail lines; use get_job_logs for that.

method.description: Method: get_workflow, get_workflow_run, get_workflow_job, download_workflow_run_artifact, get_workflow_run_usage, get_workflow_run_logs_url.

resource_id.description: Workflow id/name, workflow run id, job id, or artifact id depending on method.

tools.name: actions_get
tools.purpose: Get metadata or URLs for one GitHub Actions workflow, workflow run, job, artifact, or run usage record.
quality_checks: Set method to match the requested resource type.
Use resource_id as workflow id/name, run id, job id, or artifact id as appropriate.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks for workflow/run/job/artifact details, usage, or a logs URL by id.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid when the user only wants workflow run metadata, job metadata, artifact metadata, or a logs URL.

failed_only.description: True to fetch logs for all failed jobs in a workflow run.

job_id.description: Workflow job id for one job log.

return_content.description: True to return log content instead of URLs.

run_id.description: Workflow run id for failed_only log retrieval.

tail_lines.description: Number of tail lines to return.

tools.name: get_job_logs
tools.purpose: Return actual GitHub Actions log content for one job or for all failed jobs in a workflow run.
quality_checks: Use job_id for one job.
Use run_id plus failed_only=true for all failed jobs.
Set return_content=true when the user asks to see logs.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks to download, inspect, tail, or return workflow job logs.

cases.name: find code symbol with unknown path
cases.task: In github/github-mcp-server, find where the create_pull_request MCP tool is implemented. I do not know the file path.

cases.name: search issues by phrase
cases.task: Search github/github-mcp-server issues for reports about being unable to fetch file contents.

cases.name: read specific issue
cases.task: Get the details for issue #782 in github/github-mcp-server.

cases.name: comment on existing issue
cases.task: Add this comment to issue #782 in github/github-mcp-server: Thanks for the repro.

cases.name: create new issue
cases.task: Create a new issue in github/github-mcp-server titled "Improve MCP tool descriptions".

cases.name: list open pull requests by base branch
cases.task: List open pull requests in github/github-mcp-server targeting the main branch.

cases.name: search pull requests by author and phrase
cases.task: Find pull requests in github/github-mcp-server authored by octocat that mention CI failure.

cases.name: read changed files for specific pull request
cases.task: For PR #42 in github/github-mcp-server, show the files changed.

cases.name: create draft pull request
cases.task: Create a draft pull request in github/github-mcp-server from feature/mcp-tool-tune into main titled "Tune MCP tool descriptions".

cases.name: get failed job log content
cases.task: For workflow run 123456 in github/github-mcp-server, return the actual logs for failed jobs only.

cases.name: get workflow run metadata
cases.task: Get metadata for workflow run 123456 in github/github-mcp-server. I do not need logs.

cases.name: file not found recovers with code search
cases.task: A previous get_file_contents call for src/server.ts in github/github-mcp-server returned 404. Find the actual file path that defines the MCP server entry point.

cases.name: list pull requests with small page
cases.task: List only the first 5 open pull requests targeting main in github/github-mcp-server. Keep the result small.

cases.name: workflow metadata avoids log download
cases.task: For workflow run 123456 in github/github-mcp-server, return status, conclusion, branch, and timing metadata only. Do not download logs.

cases.name: delete repository has no safe tool
